citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
Timely information regarding a serious injury to a squirrel monkey was not provided to the attending veterinarian which
substantially delayed evaluation and treatment. The facility identified a male squirrel monkey (Simon) sustained a serious
left hip injury. The facility representative did not have an exact date of the injury but estimated sometime between late
October through early November. Review of medical notes identified that the attending veterinarian was not notified of the
serious injury until 25-November-2025, which was approximately two weeks after the injury. Radiographs performed
following the veterinarians’ recommendations determined that the primate has a luxated displaced left hip. Additionally,
the medical records demonstrate that due to the length of time between injury and evaluation and examination by a
veterinarian, correction of the displaced hip without surgery is unlikely to be successful. Failure to ensure timely and
accurate veterinary communication regarding an injury of this nature can delay treatment increasing severity, inhibit pain
management, and restrict species specific behaviors of the primate. The licensee must ensure that adequate daily
observations are made for all animals, and timely and accurate information regarding animal health, behavior, and well-
being are communicated directly and frequently to the attending veterinarian. Correct by, 14-February-2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3)  Critical
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
Timely information regarding a serious injury to a squirrel monkey was not provided to the attending veterinarian which 
substantially delayed evaluation and treatment. The facility identified a male squirrel monkey (Simon) sustained a serious 
left hip injury. The facility representative did not have an exact date of the injury but estimated sometime between late 
October through early November. Review of medical notes identified that the attending veterinarian was not notified of the 
serious injury until 25-November-2025, which was approximately two weeks after the injury. Radiographs performed 
following the veterinarians’ recommendations determined that the primate has a luxated displaced left hip. Additionally, 
the medical records demonstrate that due to the length of time between injury and evaluation and examination by a 
veterinarian, correction of the displaced hip without surgery is unlikely to be successful. Failure to ensure timely and 
accurate veterinary communication regarding an injury of this nature can delay treatment increasing severity, inhibit pain 
management, and restrict species specific behaviors of the primate. The licensee must ensure that adequate daily 
observations are made for all animals, and timely and accurate information regarding animal health, behavior, and well-
being are communicated directly and frequently to the attending veterinarian. Correct by, 14-February-2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
